evals.prompt: 幫我查一下 API Gateway Migration 這個 Obsidian 專案筆記的目前狀態和最新進度。
evals.expected_output: Claude should use read_note("Work/Projects/API-Gateway-Migration/API-Gateway-Migration.md") with a vault-relative path, NOT the Read tool with an absolute path. It should explain it cannot actually access the vault but describe the correct approach.
expectations: Uses MCP read_note tool or explicitly references read_note() — not the Read filesystem tool
Uses vault-relative path format like 'Work/Projects/...' without /Users/dustinl prefix
Does NOT attempt to use Glob, Grep, or Read filesystem tools for the vault query
Response describes or attempts the correct MCP-based lookup approach

evals.prompt: 幫我在 Obsidian 建立今天跟 Jim 和 Sarah 的 Security Review 會議記錄，主要決議是 Q1 結束前完成 PCI compliance 稽核。
evals.expected_output: Claude should use create_note() with vault-relative path Work/Meetings/YYYY/MM/Security-Review YYYY-MM-DD.md, proper frontmatter (title, date, attendees, status: draft), and correct Obsidian meeting template structure.
expectations: Uses create_note MCP tool — not Write filesystem tool
Path follows Work/Meetings/YYYY/MM/ format with vault-relative path (no /Users/dustinl prefix)
Frontmatter includes required fields: title, date, attendees, status
status field is set to 'draft' (not 'final' or any other value) for a newly created meeting note
Meeting note has proper Obsidian markdown structure with content about PCI compliance decision

evals.prompt: 幫我在 Obsidian 記錄一個技術筆記：我研究了 PostgreSQL connection pooling 的最佳實踐，主要是關於 PgBouncer 的設定方式和 pool modes 的比較。
evals.expected_output: Claude should consult note-organization.md and create the note under Dev/ (technical content), with an English filename following CamelCase/kebab-case convention, required frontmatter (title, created, updated, tags), and tags drawn from the standard vocabulary (e.g., technical, database, documentation). Should verify creation with read_note after creating.
expectations: Uses create_note MCP tool — not Write filesystem tool
Path is vault-relative under Dev/ directory (technical research belongs in Dev/, not Work/)
Frontmatter includes all four required fields: title, created, updated, tags
Tags follow the standard classification vocabulary (e.g., technical, database, documentation, draft) — not arbitrary invented tags
Verifies the note was created successfully with read_note after calling create_note

evals.prompt: 幫我更新 Work/Projects/API-Gateway-Migration/API-Gateway-Migration.md 這個專案筆記的 'Current Status' section，把內容改成：Migration completed. All services running on new gateway as of 2026-03-07.
evals.expected_output: Claude should use patch_note(path, content, heading='Current Status') to update only the specified section — NOT rewrite the whole note with create_note or use append_note.
expectations: Uses patch_note MCP tool with the heading parameter set to 'Current Status'
Does NOT use create_note (which would overwrite the whole note)
Does NOT use append_note (which appends instead of replacing a section)
Path is vault-relative: Work/Projects/API-Gateway-Migration/API-Gateway-Migration.md
Updated content mentions the completion date 2026-03-07

evals.prompt: 幫我在 Obsidian 的 Daily Note 新增一條記錄：今天成功把 auth service 的流量切換到新 gateway，整體很順利。我的 Daily Notes 都放在 DailyNote/YYYY/MM/YYYY-MM-DD.md，今天是 2026-03-07。
evals.expected_output: Even though the user explicitly mentions the DailyNote path format, Claude should still use append_periodic_note(period='daily') — NOT construct the path manually and call append_note('DailyNote/2026/03/2026-03-07.md', ...).
expectations: Uses mcp__obsidian__append_periodic_note or mcp__obsidian__patch_periodic_note with period='daily'
Does NOT call append_note with a manually-constructed path like DailyNote/2026/03/2026-03-07.md
Does NOT call create_note with a manually-constructed dated path
Content includes the key detail about auth service traffic cutover

evals.prompt: 幫我讀一下我在 Dev/Infrastructure/Kubernetes.md 存的 Kubernetes 部署策略筆記。
evals.expected_output: When read_note returns 404, Claude should call list_files on the parent directory (Dev/Infrastructure/ or Dev/) to find close matches, then read and return the correct note — not just report an error and stop.
expectations: Calls mcp__obsidian__read_note first as a reasonable first attempt
When read_note fails (404 or error), calls mcp__obsidian__list_files on a parent directory to find close matches
Informs the user of the actual path used or alternative notes found
Does NOT immediately give up after the first failure without attempting recovery
